Details: Based on available data, the addition of hydrogen peroxide alone does not result in the removal of alachlor from water.   However, chemicals such as copper oxide nanoparticles are efficient catalysts for the degradation of alachlor using hydrogen peroxide. It was also discovered that there is an apparent optimum hydrogen peroxide dose to achieve maximum removal, and addition of excess  hydrogen peroxide results in a decrease in the removal of alachlor [706].
